data.title: Alstom plunges 30% after pulling cash flow guidance
data.description:
Alstom (ALSMY) (AOMFF) sank nearly 30% in European trading Friday, on course for its worst single-day decline since October 2023, after withdrawing its financial guidance, citing slow progress on key projects for railway rolling stock.

The French train maker pulled its target for €1.5B (~$1.77B) in cumulative free cash flow for the three years until ​the end of the current 2026-27 financial year, as well as its target of an 8%-10% adjusted EBIT margin by FY 2027.

Alstom (ALSMY) (AOMFF) issued preliminary results for FY 2026 ended March 31, saying its adjusted EBIT margin fell to 6%, down from 6.4% in the previous fiscal year and lower than the 7% it previously forecast, and free cash flow declined to €330M from €502M in FY 2025.

Orders for the fiscal year jumped 42% Y/Y on an organic basis to €27.6B, while sales grew 7% organically to €19.2B.

"In a business where rigorous planning and disciplined execution are essential, some large rolling-stock projects have progressed more slowly than anticipated, weighing on near-term margins and cash," new CEO Martin Sion said, without describing which projects and regions were hit by the delays.

"We are therefore launching immediate actions to stabilize performance, while preparing deeper operational changes to restore sustainable execution, cash generation, and profitable growth," Sion said.

"The core issue is engineering execution rather than demand, with delays in homologation, German ramp-ups, and late‑stage deliveries driving cost overruns," Redburn analyst James Moore said in a note.

data.title: 4 stocks to watch on Friday: DIS, XOM, AAPL, FITB
data.description:
Stock index futures edged higher on Tuesday. Here are the four stocks to watch on the day:

Disney (DIS) rose 0.08% in premarket trade as the media giant unveiled plans for Super Bowl LXI in 2027. Under its rights deal with the NFL, the game will be telecast on both ABC and ESPN, with a separate “alterna-cast” led by Peyton and Eli Manning to appear on ESPN2. The Super Bowl is set to take place on Valentine’s Day and will be followed by a federal holiday, potentially amplifying the event’s spectacle.

Exxon Mobil (XOM) fell 1.21% before the opening bell after withdrawing an offer to sell two initial cargoes of liquefied natural gas from its Golden Pass export plant in Texas, according to Reuters. The plant has been running at roughly a third of its capacity since starting production last month, taking in ~287 million cubic feet per day of natural gas on Thursday—well below its first train’s nameplate capacity of 800 million cubic feet per day.

Fifth Third Bancorp (FITB) slipped 0.10% in premarket trading after loan loss provisions came in higher than estimated, weighing on first-quarter results. The bank reported Q1 GAAP EPS of $0.15, missing the S&P Capital IQ consensus estimate of $0.22, while revenue of $2.83 billion trailed expectations by $9 million. The company noted a net negative $0.68 impact from certain items.

Apple (AAPL) gained in premarket trade following data showing iPhone shipments rose 20% in China during the first quarter. According to preliminary figures from Counterpoint Research’s Market Monitor Tracker, the growth was driven by strong performance of the iPhone 17 series, promotional price cuts, and government subsidies. Apple ranked second in China’s smartphone market with a 19% share, just behind Huawei at 20%.

data.title: SunOpta shareholders approve buyout deal
data.description:
SunOpta (STKL) announced that its shareholders approved at a special meeting the acquisition of the company by Refresco at a deal price of $6.50 per share.  The deal values SunOpta (STKL) at $1.1B on an enterprise value basis 

The merger was approved by 98.1% of the votes cast at the special meeting, and a non-binding, advisory executive compensation proposal was approved by 82.5% of the votes cast at the special meeting.

The closing of the deal is still subject to remaining regulatory clearance or approval, approval by the Ontario Superior Court of Justice, and the satisfaction or waiver of other customary closing conditions. 

Upon completion of the transaction, SunOpta (STKL) will become a wholly owned subsidiary of Refresco. In light of the pending transaction, SunOpta (STKL) suspended its quarterly earnings conference calls and will no longer be providing quarterly or annual guidance.

SunOpta (STKL) CEO Brian Kocher said the strategic combination validates the vision of transforming SunOpta (STKL) into a premier solutions partner in the high-growth better-for-you food and beverage space. "Over the past several years, we've built exceptional platforms serving marquee customers and consistently delivering double-digit growth while maintaining the highest food safety and quality standards. This partnership with Refresco provides the resources and scale to unlock SunOpta's full potential," he added.

data.title: Private credit fears overstated, Blue Owl fundamentals intact, High Yield Investor’s Smith says
data.description:
Blue Owl Capital’s (OWL) recent selloff is overdone and does not reflect a deterioration in private credit fundamentals, Samuel Smith of High Yield Investor said on Seeking Alpha’s Investing Experts podcast.

Smith said concerns around liquidity, valuation opacity, and systemic risk have been amplified by media narratives and investor positioning, triggering elevated redemptions and slower fundraising across private credit even as underlying performance remains stable.

He added that comparisons with the 2007–2008 financial crisis are misplaced, noting that private credit vehicles operate with significantly lower leverage and rely on long-duration capital, limiting the risk of forced deleveraging or run-like dynamics.

On Blue Owl specifically, Smith said direct lending represents only part of the firm’s platform, alongside real estate, GP stakes, and digital infrastructure strategies.

While fundraising in direct lending has slowed and redemption requests have increased, he said overall assets under management have remained broadly stable due to diversification and structural features such as redemption gates.

More broadly, Smith said large alternative asset managers continue to report low non-accruals and limited realized losses. He added that the sector’s recent repricing appears driven more by sentiment and positioning than by any meaningful weakening in credit quality.

data.title: XCF Global secures $10M funding to advance plant conversion, supports planned business combination
data.description:
• XCF Global (SAFX) on Friday said it has raised $10 million from private investors through the sale of 100 million shares of common stock to fund a planned plant conversion at its New Rise Renewables Reno facility.
  • The company said the funding satisfies a key financing condition tied to its proposed three-party business combination with DevvStream and Southern Energy Renewables.
  • XCF added the transaction remains subject to several closing conditions, including shareholder approvals, regulatory clearances, stock exchange listing approvals, completion of financing and plant conversion, and other milestones.
  • DEVS -1.75% premarket to $0.6791.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: State Street Q1 earnings beat, reflecting record fees, revenue
data.description:
State Street (STT) stock rose 1.5% in Friday premarket trading after the custodial bank posted record quarterly fee revenue, net interest income, and total revenue in the first quarter of 2026.

Q1 adjusted EPS of $2.84, topping the average analyst estimate of $2.64, fell from $2.97 in Q4 2025 and rose from $2.04 in last year's Q1.

Q1 revenue of $3.80B, exceeding the $3.69B consensus, increased from $3.67B in the prior quarter and $3.28B in the year-ago period.

"We delivered record quarterly fee revenue, net interest income, and total revenue, generating meaningful year-over-year positive operating leverage and pretax margin expansion, excluding notable items," said Chairman and CEO Ron O'Hanley. "In a dynamic operating environment, the momentum across Investment Services, Investment Management, and Markets underscores the strength of our franchise."

Net interest income of $835M, beating the $784M consensus, climbed from $802M in Q4 and $714M in last year's Q1.

Total net inflows of $49B fell from $85B in the prior quarter and reversed from outflows of $13B a year ago.

Provision for credit losses of $16M increased from $8M in Q4 and $12M in Q1 2025.

Total expenses were $2.81B, up from $2.74B in the previous quarter and $2.45B a year ago.

Conference call at 11:00 AM ET.

data.title: Anthropic discussing AI models, including cybersecurity ones, with EU
data.description:
Anthropic (ANTHRO) ​is in discussion ‌with the European Commission for its different models, ​including its cyber​security ones, which are ⁠not yet available ​in the EU, Reuters reported, citing EC spokesperson Thomas Regnier.

The U.S. AI giant has already committed to respecting ​the EU's ​general purpose AI code of ‌practice, Regnier told reporters in Brussels.

"In this framework, there ​is an ​obligation ⁠to assess and mitigate risks ​that could come ​from ⁠a service that may or may not ⁠be ​offered in Europe," said Regnier, as per the report.

Anthropic and Regnier did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

Last week, Anthropic unveiled its most powerful model Claude Mythos Preview, a general-purpose frontier model, which the company called its "most capable yet for coding and agentic tasks."  The company is discussing Mythos with the Trump administration despite a legal tussle between the two after a blacklisting.

Earlier on Friday, it was reported that Anthropic's CEO Dario Amodei is set to meet White House Chief of Staff Susie Wiles in what could mark a breakthrough in the company’s dispute with the Pentagon over AI use.

Reportedly, the Trump Administration is looking to give federal agencies access to Mythos. Separately, the American Securities Association said on Thursday that the Mythos poses significant risks to individual investors through a Securities and Exchange Commission database.

Last week, Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent and Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell were said to have summoned Wall Street leaders to an urgent meeting on concerns that Mythos would usher in an era of greater cyber risk. Reportedly, U.K. financial authorities are also moving to evaluate potential threats linked to the new AI model.

Anthropic has made the model available in preview to about 50 companies, according to a report.

Anthropic had sued the U.S. Department of War after the department dropped its contract with the company and labeled it a supply chain risk. The Claude AI chatbot maker — which is backed by Amazon (AMZN) and Alphabet's (GOOG) (GOOGL) Google — had rejected the Department of War's demand for unrestricted access to its AI models.

Last week, a federal appeals court in Washington, D.C., refused, for now, to halt the Pentagon’s order to blacklist the use of Anthropic's AI technology. Another appeals court had reached the opposite outcome in a separate challenge by the company.

data.title: Stock index futures rise as Trump signals Iran war may end soon
data.description:
Stock index futures rose on Friday as President Donald Trump signaled that the U.S. and Iran could hold talks over the weekend, boosting optimism that Middle East tensions may be easing.

Dow futures (INDU) rose 0.5%, while S&P 500 futures (SPX) edged up 0.4% and Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) were up 0.4%. 

Sentiment improved after a 10-day ceasefire between Lebanon and Israel took effect, with Trump expressing confidence that an agreement could soon be reached to end the Iran conflict and urging the Tehran-aligned Hezbollah group to hold its fire.

U.S. Treasury yields edged lower across the curve. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 1.3 basis points to 4.31%, while the 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) slipped 0.4 basis points to 3.78%. The 30-year Treasury yield (US30Y) declined 1 basis point to 4.93%.

Investors were preparing for a busy schedule of Federal Reserve commentary, with speeches expected from San Francisco Fed President Mary Daly, Richmond Fed President Thomas Barkin, and Fed Governor Christopher Waller.

Top gainers in premarket trading included NiSource (NI) +2.64%, NRG Energy (NRG) +2.08%, and Aon (AON) +1.99%. Decliners included DaVita (DVA) -2.79%, FirstEnergy (FE) -1.85%, and Aflac (AFL) -1.77%.
